title: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture
description:
The Hadoop MapReduce architecture consists of the following components:
- The Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) is a distributed file system designed to run on clusters of computers. It provides high-throughput access to application data and is fault-tolerant.
- The MapReduce framework is a programming model and an associated implementation that allows for the processing of large data sets across clusters of computers. It is designed to scale up from single servers to thousands of machines, each offering local computation and storage.
- The YARN (Yet Another Resource Negotiator) resource manager is responsible for managing resources and applications in the Hadoop cluster. It coordinates the allocation of resources across different applications and provides a centralized platform for managing the cluster.
- The MapReduce job tracker is responsible for planning and monitoring MapReduce jobs. It coordinates the execution of tasks across the cluster and provides a centralized platform for managing the job.
- The TaskTracker is responsible for executing tasks assigned by the job tracker. It provides a secure, localized environment for tasks to run and reports task status and progress back to the job tracker.

The Hadoop MapReduce architecture is designed to handle large-scale data processing tasks efficiently and fault-tolerantly. It provides a scalable and flexible platform for processing data across clusters of computers.
